rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include a concise summary table of the sensitivity-analysis results in the memo (which thresholds changed feature status and which features became control-drivers) so reviewers don't need to open the workbook. Also explicitly document the exact Sensitivity sheet cells or named ranges used and attach a short note recommending SME verification of the pressure-reduction operational feasibility.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present with exact filenames. The memo reproduces the embedded packet values (F1..F4) and documents the decision rule verbatim. The immediate-rule logic (depth_pct_wall > 50 OR (HCA == Y AND MAOP_pct > 85)) is implemented and applied: F3 (depth 55) and F4 (HCA and MAOP 88) are flagged as immediate digs. The pressure-reduction proxy was applied correctly to F4: 88 * (1 - 0.25) = 66, falling below the 85 threshold and reflected in the memo and workbook. The schedule respects dig_capacity_week=2 (Week 1: F3,F4; Week 2: F1,F2). Row-level citations are present in the memo final decision table and validation metadata indicates citation_completeness=4. The trace indicates a Sensitivity sheet was generated and the checks sheet reports PASS for sensitivity/validation checks; workbook XML inspection confirmed formula-driven flags, sort keys, and schedule allocation. No external data or invented numeric values were used; the pressure-reduction proxy was applied as stated in the packet and the memo explicitly marks it as needing SME review.
